title: Brs Dry Bulk Weekly Newsletter
summary: Iron ore shipments recorded a slump of 40 million tons in 2022. This was primarily driven by losses in the Capesize (160k dwt~220k dwt), Supramax (50k dwt~68k dwt) and Panamax (68k dwt~85k dwt) categories, which saw reductions of 19 million tons, 24 million tons, and 6 million tons, respectively. In the beginning of 2023, iron ore seaborne market showed signs of recovery, achieving a historical high of 128.5 million tons of shipments for the January, which is 4.2% higher than that in January 2022.
